summary: The Nifty 50 index closed at 23842.65 on April 13, 2026, indicating a bearish sentiment for the day. The market experienced a significant decline of 224.82 contributing points, driven by heavy selling pressure. FIIs were net sellers to the tune of -1983.18 Cr, while DIIs showed net buying of 2432.30 Cr. The Advance/Decline ratio was heavily skewed towards decliners with a 10/39 split.  Top contributing stocks to the downside included HDFCBANK (-56.99 points) and RELIANCE (-52.19 points), both experiencing notable percentage drops. ICICIBANK was a lone gainer among the top contributors, adding 40.16 points.  Option chain data reveals substantial put OI build-up at the 23800 strike, suggesting this level could act as immediate support. Conversely, significant call OI additions at 23850 and 23900 indicate resistance. The market's immediate outlook appears bearish, with key levels to watch being 23800 (support) and 23850-23900 (resistance). Investors should monitor FII/DII flows and the performance of banking and heavyweight stocks for further directional cues.
